pi_items.source: GIMP official documentation https://docs.gimp.org/3.0/en/script-fu-addborder.html confirms the filter is named 'Add Border' in menu Filters → Decor → Add Border
GIMP documentation and multiple sources confirm exact menu path is 'Filters → Decor → Add Border' per https://docs.gimp.org/3.0/en/script-fu-addborder.html

pi_items.metadata_value: Borders expand image dimensions rather than overlay existing content
pi_items.verified_value: GIMP's Add Border filter expands image dimensions - image size is increased by twice the specified border size (e.g., 30px border adds 60px total to each dimension, 30px on each side)
pi_items.source: Multiple sources confirm Add Border expands dimensions: https://docs.gimp.org/3.0/en/script-fu-addborder.html and search results describing X and Y size parameters as border thickness

pi_items.metadata_value: Task uses real-world image data from Unsplash with fallback to synthetic image generation
pi_items.verified_value: Real - both Unsplash URLs are valid and accessible; setup script has fallback to ImageMagick-generated test image only if downloads fail
pi_items.source: Verified by fetching both URLs and examining setup_border_task.sh fallback logic

privileged_info_summary: The task attempts to use GIMP's border filter but has a critical nomenclature error: the task description and README refer to the filter as 'Filters → Decor → Border', but the actual GIMP filter is called 'Filters → Decor → Add Border'. This could cause agent confusion when navigating GIMP menus. The two Unsplash image URLs are valid and resolve to landscape photographs. The Add Border filter does expand image dimensions as claimed, with the expansion being twice the specified border size. The task data is real (from Unsplash) with a synthetic fallback. The verifier's dimension thresholds (20-200px increase) and color uniformity checks are reasonable for detecting successfully applied borders.
